turns.text: Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you today?
Yeah, I've got water just POURING out of my kitchen ceiling right now and I've been on hold for ten minutes already.
Oh no, water coming through your ceiling — that sounds really urgent. Can I grab your name real quick?
It's Dave. Dave Morton. And I need someone here NOW, not in three days or whatever you people usually do.
Dave, I totally understand the urgency. Let me see what we've got for emergency availability right now.
I called you guys last year and it took TWO DAYS for someone to show up for an "emergency." Two days! My whole kitchen floor was ruined.
Oh gosh, I'm so sorry about that experience, Dave. That's not okay. We do have an on-call tech today — what's your address?
You know what, forget it. I'm calling someone else. I can't trust you people to actually show up.
Dave, I completely understand your frustration, and I —
[caller hangs up]
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! What can I do for you?
Hi, um, yeah I've got... there's something going on with my, uh... the thing outside? In the yard?
Okay, something going on in your yard — can you tell me a little more about what you're seeing?
It's like... wet? But it hasn't rained. And there's a smell. Like a bad smell. I don't really know what it is.
Got it — so a wet spot in the yard with a bad smell. That could be a sewer line issue. Is the wet area near where your main drain line runs?
I don't... I don't know where the drain line is. I don't know any of this stuff. My husband used to handle all this but he's not... he's not here anymore.
That's totally okay, you don't need to know any of that. We can send someone out to take a look and figure it all out. Can I get your name?
It's Margaret. But I just... I don't even know if this is the right place to call. Maybe I should call the city? Or... I don't know.
Margaret, you called the right place! We deal with this kind of thing all the time. We'd just come take a look and tell you exactly what's going on.
I... okay, but I need to think about it. I'm sorry, I'm just overwhelmed. I think I need to call my son first.
No need to apologize at all, Margaret. You can call us back anytime — we're here Monday through Saturday.
Okay. Thank you. I'm sorry. Bye.
No worries at all. Take care, Margaret!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing! This is Sarah, how can I help?
Hello, yes, I have... the water hot machine? Is not working.
Your water heater — okay! So no hot water coming out?
No no, the water is come out but is... how you say... not hot. Only little warm. And is making noise.
Oh okay, so lukewarm water and it's making noise. We can definitely help with that! Can I get your name?
My name is Yun-seo. Y-U-N, then S-E-O.
Yun-seo, got it! And what's your address?
Is, um... 4-4-7... wait. Sorry, my English is not so good. The address is 447... Maple? Maple Street.
447 Maple Street — perfect. We could send someone out Thursday morning, does that work for you?
Thursday... what is Thursday? Sorry, I am not understand. What day?
No problem! Thursday is in two days. The day after tomorrow.
Two days? Oh... I think maybe I ask my neighbor to help me call. She speak better English. I call back, okay?
Of course, Yun-seo! Call back anytime. We're happy to help.
Thank you. Sorry. Bye bye.
No need to apologize! Bye now!
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you today?
Hi, I have an appointment scheduled for tomorrow and I need to cancel it.
Oh sure, I can help with that. Can I get the name on the appointment?
It's under Brian Kowalski. It was for a leak under my bathroom sink.
Okay, I see it right here — Brian Kowalski, leak repair, tomorrow at 10 AM. Before I cancel, is there anything we can do? Maybe reschedule to a better time?
No, I actually got my brother-in-law to come fix it this weekend, so I don't need the appointment anymore.
Oh nice, that works out! Alright Brian, I've got that canceled for you. If it gives you any more trouble, just give us a call.
Will do. Thanks, Sarah.
You're welcome, Brian! Have a great day.
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help?
Yeah, I need to cancel my appointment and honestly I'm done with you guys.
Oh no, I'm sorry to hear that. Can I get your name so I can pull up the appointment?
Karen Albrecht. I had a toilet repair scheduled for Friday. But I'm canceling because your guy never showed up LAST time.
Oh gosh, Karen, I'm really sorry about that. A no-show is completely unacceptable.
You're right it's unacceptable! I took a whole day off work. Sat here waiting. Nobody came, nobody called. That's just disrespectful.
You're absolutely right, and I completely understand why you're upset. Is there anything we could do to make this right? We could prioritize your appointment and I'd personally follow up to confirm.
No. I've already called Roto-Rooter and they're coming Tuesday. Cancel the Friday appointment. I'm not going through that again.
I understand, Karen. I've canceled your Friday appointment. I'm truly sorry about your experience — that's not the standard we hold ourselves to.
Well, maybe you should tell your technicians that. Goodbye.
I will. Take care, Karen.
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! What can I do for you?

Oh hi Sarah! So I've been doing this whole kitchen renovation — you should see it, we picked out these gorgeous quartz countertops, and my husband wanted granite but I said no way, quartz is so much easier to maintain, you know?
Ha, oh nice! Quartz is beautiful. So what can we help you with today?

Right, right! So we actually had an appointment to install a new kitchen faucet — it's this really pretty brushed gold one from Delta, it was on sale at Home Depot, can you believe it was forty percent off? Anyway, I'm calling about that appointment.
Oh wow, great deal! Let me pull that up — what's the name on it?

Patty Delgado. D-E-L-G-A-D-O. So the thing is, and I feel just terrible about this, but my neighbor — she's a sweetheart, her name's Linda, she lives two doors down — her son is actually a plumber and he offered to do it for like half the price.
Oh I see! So you're looking to cancel the faucet install, Patty?

Yeah, I mean, I hate to do it because you guys were so nice when I called the first time, but with everything we're spending on the renovation — the countertops alone were like four thousand dollars — I just can't say no to saving a few hundred bucks, you know?
Oh totally, I get it! Every dollar counts with a big reno. I've got that canceled for you, Patty. If Linda's son runs into any trouble, we're always here!
Aw, you're so sweet! I'll definitely keep you guys in mind. We still need to redo the master bathroom eventually, so you'll probably hear from me again. Okay, thanks Sarah! Bye!
Sounds great, Patty! Good luck with the reno. Bye now!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing! This is Sarah, how can I help you today?
Yes... hello? Is this the plumber?
Yes ma'am, this is Smith Plumbing! What can I help you with?
Oh good, good. Well, I have a question. My kitchen sink is draining very slowly and I was wondering... how much does it cost to have someone come look at that?
Oh sure! So for a clogged drain, our service call is $89 and that includes the first hour of work.
I'm sorry, how much did you say? Eighty-nine?
Yes ma'am — $89 for the service call.
And that includes them actually fixing it? Or is that just to come look at it?
That includes the first hour of work, so usually a basic drain clearing is covered in that. If it turns out to be something bigger, the tech would let you know before doing any extra work.
Okay, okay. So eighty-nine dollars, and that covers the first hour. And they won't do anything extra without telling me first?
That's exactly right! No surprises.
Alright, well, let me think about it. I might have my grandson try the plunger first. Can I get your name again, dear?
Of course! I'm Sarah. Call back anytime.
Sarah. Alright, thank you, Sarah. You've been very helpful. Goodbye now.
You're welcome! Bye now, take care!
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help?
Hello, yes. I have question. The machine in the sink? For the food? Is broken.
The garbage disposal — the thing that grinds up the food in the sink?
Yes! Yes, that one. The disposal. You fix this?
Yes, we sure do! We can fix or replace garbage disposals. Would you like to schedule someone to come out?
How much? I want to know price first.
Sure thing! The service call is $89, and if it needs to be replaced, the disposal unit itself is usually between $150 and $300 depending on the model.
So maybe... $89 plus $300? Is almost $400?
In that range, yeah, if it needs a full replacement. But sometimes it's just a quick fix and it's just the $89.
Okay. I ask my wife and call back. What is your hours?
We're open Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM, and Saturdays 9 to 1.
Monday to Friday, 8 to 5. Okay, thank you very much.
You're welcome! Call back anytime. Bye!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing! This is Sarah, how can I —
Hey, quick question — do you guys work on Rheem water heaters?
Yep! We work on all major brands — Rheem, AO Smith, Bradford White, you name it.
Great. And tankless too? I've got a Rheem tankless.
Absolutely, tankless too.
Perfect. What's a service call run?
$89 for the service call, includes the first hour.
Got it. Alright, I might call back. Thanks.
Sounds good! We're here whenever you need us.
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you today?
I've got a leak under my bathroom sink that's been going on for a week now and I called another plumber who blew me off. I'm about done with all of you.
Ugh, I'm sorry you're dealing with that — a week is a long time to have a leak going. Can I get your name?
Rick. Rick Tanner. But honestly, I'm about to just YouTube it and do it myself. You people charge an arm and a leg and then don't even show up.
I don't blame you for feeling that way, Rick. Getting blown off is the worst. We do things differently — we confirm the day before and the tech calls when he's on his way.
Yeah, that's what the last guy said too.
I hear you. Tell you what — if for any reason our tech doesn't show up in the scheduled window, we'll waive the service call fee entirely. How's that sound?
...You'd actually do that?
Absolutely. We stand behind our word, Rick.
Alright, fine. Let's do it. When can you get someone out here?
How does Wednesday morning work? We've got a 9 to 11 window.
Wednesday works. I'm at 218 Birch Lane.
Got it — Rick Tanner, 218 Birch Lane, Wednesday morning between 9 and 11 for the leak under your bathroom sink. What's a good phone number for you?
Perfect. So just to confirm — Rick Tanner, 218 Birch Lane, Wednesday 9 to 11 AM, leak repair. We'll call you the day before to confirm and the tech will call when he's headed your way.
Alright. He better show up.
He will, Rick. I promise. Have a good one!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing! This is Sarah, how can I help?
Hi, um, so I've got kind of a weird situation. There's like, a gurgling sound when I flush my toilet? And the shower drain is slow too. I don't know if those are related.
Oh yeah, actually those usually are related! That sounds like it could be a main sewer line issue. Can I get your name?
Tom. Tom Nguyen. But I don't really know if I need a plumber or... like, is this something I should call the city about?
Good question, Tom! If it's happening inside your house, it's usually on your side, not the city's. We can come out and do a camera inspection to see exactly what's going on.
A camera inspection? What does that involve? Is that expensive?
So we run a small camera through your drain line to see if there's a blockage or damage. It's $189 for the inspection, and then we'd know exactly what we're dealing with.
Hmm, I don't know. That seems like a lot just to look at it. Maybe I should just wait and see if it gets worse.

I totally get that, Tom. The only thing I'd mention is that sewer line stuff can sometimes get worse pretty fast — what starts as gurgling can turn into a backup. The camera lets us catch it before it becomes a bigger problem.
A backup? Like, sewage backing up into my house?
It can happen, yeah. Not trying to scare you! But it's better to know what you're working with.
Okay, yeah, let's do it. I don't want sewage in my house. When can you come?
How about Thursday afternoon? We've got a 1 to 3 window.
Thursday afternoon... yeah, I can make that work. I'm at 95 Willow Court.
Great! And a phone number for you, Tom?
Alright, Tom Nguyen, 95 Willow Court, Thursday between 1 and 3 PM for a sewer line camera inspection. We'll give you a call the day before to confirm. Sound good?
Yeah, sounds good. Thanks for explaining all that, Sarah.
Of course, Tom! We'll get it figured out for you. Have a great day!
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help?
Hey, I need someone to fix my toilet ASAP. It's running nonstop. Can you get someone here tomorrow?
Running toilet, got it! Let me check tomorrow's schedule real quick. Can I get your name?
Mike Reeves. So can you do tomorrow or not?
So unfortunately tomorrow's completely booked up, Mike. The soonest I've got is Thursday.
Thursday? That's two days away. This thing is running 24/7, it's gonna cost me a fortune in water.
I totally understand, Mike. If it helps, you can turn the shut-off valve behind the toilet to stop the water until we get there. Just turn it clockwise.
Yeah, I guess. What times on Thursday?
I've got an 8 to 10 AM window — first thing in the morning.
I have a meeting at 9. What about afternoon?
Thursday afternoon is full, but I could do Friday morning 8 to 10?
That's even further out! Ugh. Fine, put me down for Thursday morning. I'll move my meeting.
Okay, Mike Reeves, Thursday 8 to 10 AM. What's your address?
1440 Oak Ridge Drive. Phone is 469-555-0221. Gotta go.
Got it — Mike Reeves, 1440 Oak Ridge Drive, Thursday 8 to 10 AM for the running toilet. Don't forget that shut-off valve! Take care, Mike.
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you today?
My kitchen sink has been backed up since yesterday and I need someone here today.
Oh no, backed up since yesterday — that's no fun. Let me check today's availability. Can I get your name?
Lisa Hartman. And I already tried Drano, I tried the plunger, nothing works.
Okay Lisa, so unfortunately today is completely full. The earliest I have is Friday morning.
Friday?! That's three days from now! I can't use my kitchen sink for three days!
I know, that's really frustrating. I can put you on our cancellation list so if anything opens up sooner, you'd be first in line.
A cancellation list. So I just sit here and hope somebody cancels? This is ridiculous.
I completely understand, Lisa. We do get cancellations pretty regularly, so there's a good chance something opens up. Can I go ahead and book you for Friday as a backup and put you on that list?
You know what, no. If you can't get someone here before Friday, I'll find someone who can. This is unbelievable.
I'm really sorry we couldn't get you in sooner, Lisa. If you change your mind, we're here — just give us a call.
[caller hangs up]
Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you today?
Hello, dear. I need to have a new faucet put in my bathroom. The old one has been dripping for months now.
Oh sure, we can help with a faucet install! Can I get your name?
It's Dorothy. Dorothy Schafer. S-C-H-A-F-E-R.
Got it, Dorothy! Do you already have the new faucet, or do you need us to bring one?
Oh, my daughter bought one for me. It's in the box. I was hoping you could come Monday — that's when my daughter can be here to let you in.
Let me check Monday for you... so unfortunately Monday is all booked up, Dorothy. I've got openings on Wednesday or Thursday.
Oh... not Monday? My daughter works the rest of the week. She's the one who has to be here because I can't get down the stairs to open the door.
Oh I see. Hmm, what about the following Monday? Would that work?
The next Monday... let me think. What date would that be?
That would be the 20th. Monday the 20th.
The 20th. And what time would that be?
I've got a morning slot, 10 to 12, or afternoon 2 to 4. Which sounds better?
Oh my, let me think... my daughter gets off at... I think the afternoon one. The 2 to 4. Can you say that again?
Monday the 20th, between 2 and 4 PM.
Monday the 20th, 2 to 4. Yes, I think that works. Let me give you my address — it's 77 Elm Street, Apartment 3B.
77 Elm Street, Apartment 3B. And what's a good phone number, Dorothy?
My phone number is 817-555-0143. That's my home phone. Should I also give you my daughter's number?
That would be great, actually, since she'll be letting us in!
Her name is Janet, and her number is 817-555-0299.
Perfect. So just to make sure I've got everything right — Dorothy Schafer, 77 Elm Street, Apartment 3B, Monday the 20th between 2 and 4 PM for a faucet install. Your daughter Janet will let us in.
Yes, that's right. Thank you so much, dear. You've been very patient with me.
Oh, not at all, Dorothy! We'll see you on the 20th. Have a wonderful day!
Thanks for calling Smith Plumbing, this is Sarah! How can I help you?
Hello, yes, please, I smell gas. In my house. Smell gas.
You smell gas — okay, I need you to leave the house right now, immediately. Take your family and go outside. Can you do that?
Leave? Okay, I go outside. But the gas — you can fix?
First thing is getting you safe. Are you outside now?
Yes, yes, I am outside now. With my children.
Good, stay outside. Now I need you to call 911 right away — that's the emergency number. Tell them you smell gas. They'll send the fire department. Can you do that?
9-1-1? Okay. But the gas company, they fix? Or you fix?
The fire department will make sure it's safe first. Then the gas company will come check. We can help if there's a plumbing issue after, but right now just call 911, okay?
Okay. I call 911 now. Thank you.
Yes, call them right now. And don't go back inside until they say it's safe. What's your name so I can follow up?
My name is Amir. Amir Hassan.
Okay Amir, call 911 right now. Once everything is safe, call us back and we'll help with whatever you need. Stay safe!
Thank you, thank you. I call now. Bye.
